A macro to ergonomically define an item depending on a large number of #[cfg]
parameters. Structured like an if-else chain, the first matching branch is the
item that gets emitted.

packages.name: chacha20
packages.description:
The ChaCha20 stream cipher (RFC 8439) implemented in pure Rust using traits
from the RustCrypto `cipher` crate, with optional architecture-specific
hardware acceleration (AVX2, SSE2). Additionally provides the ChaCha8, ChaCha12,
XChaCha20, XChaCha12 and XChaCha8 stream ciphers, and also optional
rand_core-compatible RNGs based on those ciphers.

packages.name: chacha20poly1305
packages.description:
Pure Rust implementation of the ChaCha20Poly1305 Authenticated Encryption
with Additional Data Cipher (RFC 8439) with optional architecture-specific
hardware acceleration. Also contains implementations of the XChaCha20Poly1305
extended nonce variant of ChaCha20Poly1305, and the reduced-round
ChaCha8Poly1305 and ChaCha12Poly1305 lightweight variants.

packages.name: ipnet
packages.description: Provides types and useful methods for working with IPv4 and IPv6 network addresses, commonly called IP prefixes. The new `IpNet`, `Ipv4Net`, and `Ipv6Net` types build on the existing `IpAddr`, `Ipv4Addr`, and `Ipv6Addr` types already provided in Rust's standard library and align to their design to stay consistent. The module also provides useful traits that extend `Ipv4Addr` and `Ipv6Addr` with methods for `Add`, `Sub`, `BitAnd`, and `BitOr` operations. The module only uses stable feature so it is guaranteed to compile using the stable toolchain.

packages.name: scopeguard
packages.description:
A RAII scope guard that will run a given closure when it goes out of scope,
even if the code between panics (assuming unwinding panic).

Defines the macros `defer!`, `defer_on_unwind!`, `defer_on_success!` as
shorthands for guards with one of the implemented strategies.

packages.description: Implementation detail of the `thiserror` crate

packages.name: thiserror-impl
packages.description: Implementation detail of the `thiserror` crate

packages.name: thread_local
packages.description: Per-object thread-local storage

packages.name: time
packages.description: Date and time library. Fully interoperable with the standard library. Mostly compatible with #![no_std].

packages.name: time-core
packages.description: This crate is an implementation detail and should not be relied upon directly.

packages.name: time-macros
packages.description:
Procedural macros for the time crate.
    This crate is an implementation detail and should not be relied upon directly.

packages.name: tinystr
packages.description: A small ASCII-only bounded length string representation.

packages.name: tinyvec
packages.description: `tinyvec` provides 100% safe vec-like data structures.

packages.name: tinyvec_macros
packages.description: Some macros for tiny containers

packages.name: tokio
packages.description:
An event-driven, non-blocking I/O platform for writing asynchronous I/O
backed applications.

packages.name: tokio-macros
packages.description: Tokio's proc macros.

packages.name: tokio-util
packages.description: Additional utilities for working with Tokio.

packages.name: tracing
packages.description: Application-level tracing for Rust.

packages.name: tracing-attributes
packages.description: Procedural macro attributes for automatically instrumenting functions.

packages.name: tracing-core
packages.description: Core primitives for application-level tracing.

packages.name: tracing-log
packages.description: Provides compatibility between `tracing` and the `log` crate.

packages.name: tracing-subscriber
packages.description: Utilities for implementing and composing `tracing` subscribers.

packages.name: try-lock
packages.description: A lightweight atomic lock.

packages.name: typenum
packages.description:
Typenum is a Rust library for type-level numbers evaluated at
    compile time. It currently supports bits, unsigned integers, and signed
    integers. It also provides a type-level array of type-level numbers, but its
    implementation is incomplete.

packages.name: unicode-ident
packages.description: Determine whether characters have the XID_Start or XID_Continue properties according to Unicode Standard Annex #31

packages.name: unicode-segmentation
packages.description:
This crate provides Grapheme Cluster, Word and Sentence boundaries
according to Unicode Standard Annex #29 rules.

packages.name: unicode-xid
packages.description:
Determine whether characters have the XID_Start
or XID_Continue properties according to
Unicode Standard Annex #31.

packages.name: universal-hash
packages.description: Traits which describe the functionality of universal hash functions (UHFs)

packages.name: untrusted
packages.description: Safe, fast, zero-panic, zero-crashing, zero-allocation parsing of untrusted inputs in Rust.

packages.name: url
packages.description: URL library for Rust, based on the WHATWG URL Standard

packages.name: utf8_iter
packages.description: Iterator by char over potentially-invalid UTF-8 in &[u8]

packages.name: valuable
packages.description: Object-safe value inspection, used to pass un-typed structured data across trait-object boundaries.

packages.name: value-bag
packages.description: Anonymous structured values

packages.name: version_check
packages.description: Tiny crate to check the version of the installed/running rustc.

packages.name: want
packages.description: Detect when another Future wants a result.

packages.description: Experimental WASI API bindings for Rust

packages.description: WASIp2 API bindings for Rust

packages.name: wasm-bindgen-futures
packages.description: Bridging the gap between Rust Futures and JavaScript Promises

packages.name: wasm-bindgen-macro
packages.description: Definition of the `#[wasm_bindgen]` attribute, an internal dependency

packages.name: wasm-bindgen-macro-support
packages.description: Implementation APIs for the `#[wasm_bindgen]` attribute

packages.name: wasm-bindgen-shared
packages.description:
Shared support between wasm-bindgen and wasm-bindgen cli, an internal
dependency.

packages.description: Mozilla's CA root certificates for use with webpki

packages.description:
Rust bindings generator and runtime support for WIT and the component model.

packages.name: writeable
packages.description: A more efficient alternative to fmt::Display

packages.name: x25519-dalek
packages.description: X25519 elliptic curve Diffie-Hellman key exchange in pure-Rust, using curve25519-dalek.

packages.name: x509-parser
packages.description: Parser for the X.509 v3 format (RFC 5280 certificates)

packages.name: yoke
packages.description: Abstraction allowing borrowed data to be carried along with the backing data it borrows from

packages.name: yoke-derive
packages.description: Custom derive for the yoke crate

packages.name: zerocopy
packages.description: Zerocopy makes zero-cost memory manipulation effortless. We write "unsafe" so you don't have to.

packages.name: zerocopy-derive
packages.description: Custom derive for traits from the zerocopy crate

packages.name: zerofrom
packages.description: ZeroFrom trait for constructing

packages.name: zerofrom-derive
packages.description: Custom derive for the zerofrom crate

packages.name: zeroize
packages.description:
Securely clear secrets from memory with a simple trait built on
stable Rust primitives which guarantee memory is zeroed using an
operation will not be 'optimized away' by the compiler.
Uses a portable pure Rust implementation that works everywhere,
even WASM!

packages.name: zeroize_derive
packages.description: Custom derive support for zeroize

packages.name: zerotrie
packages.description: A data structure that efficiently maps strings to integers

packages.name: zerovec
packages.description: Zero-copy vector backed by a byte array

packages.name: zerovec-derive
packages.description: Custom derive for the zerovec crate

packages.name: zmij
packages.description: A double-to-string conversion algorithm based on Schubfach and yy
